goals: Expose all ROADMAP latent utility nodes as clean Python functions in `comfy_diffusion/latent.py`
Follow established project patterns: lazy imports, `str | Path` annotations, no top-level torch/comfy imports
All functions must be testable on CPU-only environments

userStories.title: Create an empty latent image
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to create an empty latent image with specified width, height, and batch size so that I can use it as the starting point for txt2img generation.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `empty_latent_image(width, height, batch_size=1)` returns a latent dict compatible with `sample()`
Width and height are divided by 8 internally (latent space downscale factor)
Default batch size is 1
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Upscale a latent
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to upscale a latent by target dimensions or by a scale factor so that I can do multi-pass hi-res generation.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_upscale(latent, method, width, height)` returns an upscaled latent dict
Supported methods mirror ComfyUI: `nearest-exact`, `bilinear`, `area`, `bicubic`, `bislerp`
`latent_upscale_by(latent, method, scale_by)` returns a latent scaled by a float factor
Output latent is compatible with `sample()`
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Crop a region from a latent
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to crop a rectangular region from a latent so that I can isolate a section for targeted generation or compositing.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_crop(latent, x, y, width, height)` returns a cropped latent dict
Coordinates and dimensions are in pixel space (divided by 8 internally)
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Composite one latent onto another
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to composite a source latent onto a destination latent at a given position so that I can combine independently generated regions.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_composite(destination, source, x, y)` returns a new latent with source placed at (x, y)
Coordinates are in pixel space (divided by 8 internally)
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Composite a latent using a mask
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to composite a source latent onto a destination using a mask so that I can blend regions with soft boundaries.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_composite_masked(destination, source, mask, x=0, y=0)` returns a blended latent
Mask is a torch tensor controlling blend strength per pixel
Coordinates default to (0, 0)
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Set a noise mask on a latent
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to set a noise mask on a latent so that the sampler only denoises the masked region (inpainting).

acceptanceCriteria.text: `set_latent_noise_mask(latent, mask)` returns a latent dict with `noise_mask` set
Mask is a torch tensor
Output is compatible with `sample()` for inpainting workflows
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Batch operations
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to extract a subset of frames from a latent batch and repeat a latent N times so that I can manipulate batches for video and multi-image workflows.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_from_batch(latent, batch_index, length=1)` extracts a contiguous slice from the batch dimension
`repeat_latent_batch(latent, amount)` repeats the latent along the batch dimension
Both return latent dicts compatible with `sample()`
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Video latent operations
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to concatenate latents, cut a batch into segments, and replace frames in a video latent so that I can build video generation pipelines.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `latent_concat(*latents, dim="t")` concatenates multiple latents along the specified dimension (supports `"x"`, `"-x"`, `"y"`, `"-y"`, `"t"`, `"-t"`)
`latent_cut_to_batch(latent, start, length)` extracts a segment from a batch
`replace_video_latent_frames(latent, replacement, start_frame)` replaces frames in-place starting at a given index
All return latent dicts compatible with `sample()`
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Inpaint conditioning helpers
userStories.description: As a Python developer, I want to encode an image for inpainting and set up inpaint model conditioning so that I can run inpainting pipelines end-to-end.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `vae_encode_for_inpaint(vae, image, mask, grow_mask_by=6)` encodes an image with an inpaint mask applied, returning a latent with noise mask set
`inpaint_model_conditioning(model, latent, vae, positive, negative)` returns patched (model, positive, negative) tuple for inpaint-specific models
Both are compatible with existing `sample()` and conditioning functions
Typecheck / lint passes

functionalRequirements.description: All functions use lazy imports — no `torch` or `comfy.*` at module top level
Latent dicts use the `{"samples": tensor}` format established by ComfyUI, optionally with `"noise_mask"` and `"batch_index"` keys
Pixel-space coordinates/dimensions are converted to latent space (÷8) internally; callers always think in pixels
All functions must work on CPU tensors for testing purposes
Public API symbols are importable via `from comfy_diffusion.latent import <function>`
`latent_concat` accepts variadic `*latents` and a `dim` parameter (`"x"`, `"-x"`, `"y"`, `"-y"`, `"t"`, `"-t"`) for concatenation along any spatial or temporal axis
